title: 2026 U.S. Senate election in Maine
description: Maine’s 2026 U.S. Senate race is for an open-seat-style general election contest in which Republican incumbent Susan Collins seeks another term against a Democratic field led by Gov. Janet Mills and Graham Platner. The race matters because Collins is one of the GOP’s most durable incumbents in a state that often splits tickets, making Maine a top Democratic pickup target. Democrats are weighing a more established statewide figure in Mills against Platner, a newer candidate who has drawn strong early primary and general-election interest. The general election is likely to hinge on Collins’s long record and crossover appeal versus Democrats’ argument that the state is ready for change.

polling.pollster: University of New Hampshire Survey Center

candidates.name: Susan Collins
candidates.summary: Susan Collins is the Republican senior U.S. senator from Maine and has served in the Senate since 1997. She is running for reelection in 2026 and has said that, if reelected, the next term would be her last.

summary_sources.title: Republican US Senator Susan Collins announces bid for re-election
Sen. Susan Collins says if she wins in November, it'll be her last term

Healthcare.stance: Collins says the health care system needs substantial reform and has backed bipartisan efforts to lower prescription drug costs and expand access to coverage. Her Senate issue page highlights support for reforming health care while addressing affordability concerns.

sources.title: Health Care | U.S. Senator Susan Collins
Senator Collins Championed 5 of 15 Health Care Bills Signed into Law in the 118th Congress

Economy.stance: Collins emphasizes infrastructure investment, workforce development, and support for small businesses as drivers of economic growth. Her office says she has used her appropriations role to secure federal funding for Maine projects and jobs.

sources.title: Senator Collins Champions $65 Million for Maine’s Rural Infrastructure
Jobs & Economy | U.S. Senator Susan Collins

Climate/Energy.stance: Collins says she supports reasonable environmental protections, reducing greenhouse gas emissions, and expanding clean energy jobs while also keeping energy costs manageable for Maine households and businesses. Her issue page stresses balancing environmental goals with manufacturing and affordability concerns.

sources.title: Energy & Environment | U.S. Senator Susan Collins

Reproductive Rights.stance: Collins has supported legislation to codify Roe v. Wade and Planned Parenthood v. Casey, while also describing herself as open to some abortion restrictions and bipartisan compromise. Her public record is generally viewed as more moderate than many Republicans on abortion policy.

sources.title: Senator Collins Joins Bipartisan Effort to Codify Roe v. Wade
Senators Collins and Murkowski Introduce Bill to Codify Supreme Court Decisions on Reproductive Rights
Senator Collins' Statement on SCOTUS' Dobbs Decision

Immigration.stance: Collins supports border security and immigration enforcement, and her office has highlighted efforts to require DHS reporting on border issues and to address ICE operations in Maine. She has also urged a more measured approach to enforcement in some cases.

sources.title: Homeland Security | U.S. Senator Susan Collins
Collins-Sponsored Bill to Require DHS to Report Data on Border Crisis Passes Senate
Senator Collins' Statement on ICE Operations in Maine

Guns & Safety.stance: Collins supports bipartisan gun-safety measures, including red-flag style interventions and crisis-intervention policies. She has backed legislation such as the Bipartisan Safer Communities Act and related proposals aimed at preventing gun violence while preserving lawful gun ownership.

sources.title: Senators Collins, King Reintroduce Red Flag Bill to Prevent Gun Violence While Protecting
From the Senate Floor: Collins Urges Colleagues to Support Armed Forces Crisis Intervention Notification Act

Foreign Policy.stance: Collins is a mainstream Republican on foreign policy and national security who has emphasized U.S. engagement, intelligence oversight, and support for allies and security partnerships. Her Senate committee roles and public statements point to an active international posture rather than isolationism.

sources.title: Defense & National Security | U.S. Senator Susan Collins
Senator Collins Makes Case for National Security Supplemental

Social Justice.stance: Collins has supported bipartisan measures on civil rights and anti-discrimination, including marriage equality legislation and criminal justice reform efforts. Her public record suggests an incremental, bipartisan approach rather than a broad social-justice platform.

sources.title: Respect for Marriage Act Floor Speech
Senator Collins Joins Bipartisan Group Introducing National Criminal Justice Commission Act
Sen. Susan M. Collins George Floyd statement June 1, 2020

Education.stance: Collins says she supports early childhood education, K-12 schools, and making higher education more affordable. Her office says she has used her appropriations role to secure funding for Maine schools, universities, and student support programs.

sources.title: Education | U.S. Senator Susan Collins

Tech & AI.stance: Collins supports limits on deceptive uses of artificial intelligence, especially in elections and robocalls. She has backed bipartisan proposals to provide AI guidance for election offices and to ban AI-generated robocalls.

sources.title: Senators Collins, Klobuchar Introduce Bipartisan Bill to Provide AI Guidelines for Election Offices
Senator Collins Statement on FCC Declaring AI-Generated Robocalls Illegal

Election Reform.stance: Collins has backed stricter voting rules, including voter ID and proof-of-citizenship requirements tied to the SAVE Act. She says these measures are intended to prevent fraud and strengthen election integrity.

sources.title: In Maine, Sen. Collins explains support for Republican voting reform bill
Susan Collins backs GOP's strict voting bill driven by Trump's fraud claims
Sen. Collins supports voter ID requirements approved by US House

Local Issues.stance: Collins emphasizes Maine-specific priorities such as protecting the lobster and fishing industries, working waterfronts, and coastal economies. She has highlighted federal funding and legislation aimed at supporting Maine fishermen and seafood businesses.

sources.title: Bill to Support Maine’s Lobster Industry Heads to President’s Desk
Protecting Our Waterfronts
Maine Delegation Secures $17.1 Million to Support Jobs in Maine’s Lobster Industry

candidates.donor_summary: OpenSecrets shows Susan Collins’ 2019–2024 Senate cycle fundraising is dominated by traditional Washington donor sectors, with top industry support including pro-Israel, finance/insurance, health professionals, and defense-related interests. Her campaign reported roughly $29.8M raised in the 2020 cycle, and later 2026 reporting/news coverage indicated more than $8M cash on hand and about $1.6M in a recent quarter; the best full donor data source is OpenSecrets. Sources: https://www.opensecrets.org/members-of-congress/susan-collins/summary?cid=N00000491 ; https://www.opensecrets.org/members-of-congress/susan-collins/industries?cid=N00000491&cycle=2024&type=P ; https://www.opensecrets.org/members-of-congress/susan-collins/elections?cid=N00000491&cycle=2024 ; https://www.newscentermaine.com/article/news/local/senator-susan-collins-led-democratic-challengers-in-fundraising-entering-2026/97-32582488-380f-48af-8827-8812275ad02a ; https://www.quiverquant.com/news/Fundraising+Update%3A+Senator+Susan+M.+Collins+just+disclosed+%241.6M+of+new+fundraising

links.title: Sen. Susan Collins - Campaign Finance Summary - OpenSecrets
Susan Collins' Voting Records - Vote Smart
Voting Record | U.S. Senator Susan Collins

candidates.voting_summary: Susan Collins is an incumbent U.S. senator and has served in Congress since 1997. Vote Smart and GovTrack show a long Senate voting record; her public record is generally aligned with the Republican caucus, though she has also broken with her party on some high-profile votes. Sources: https://www.govtrack.us/congress/members/susan_collins/300025 ; https://justfacts.votesmart.org/candidate/key-votes/379/susan-collins ; https://www.collins.senate.gov/about/voting-record ; https://www.congress.gov/member/susan-collins/C001035

candidates.name: Janet T. Mills
candidates.summary: Janet T. Mills is the Democratic governor of Maine and a former state attorney general and legislator. She is running for the U.S. Senate in 2026, challenging Republican incumbent Susan Collins.

summary_sources.title: About the Governor | Office of Governor Janet T. Mills - Maine.gov

career_history.title: Governor of Maine

career_history.description: Served in the Maine House before becoming attorney general.

career_history.title: District Attorney
career_history.description: Served as district attorney and was the first woman district attorney in New England.

career_history.title: Assistant Attorney General
career_history.description: Prosecuted homicide and other major crimes.

education.institution: University of Maine School of Law

Healthcare.stance: Mills says she wants to expand affordable, high-quality health care and has highlighted her record of expanding coverage in Maine. Her campaign materials and governor biography emphasize health care access as a core priority.

sources.title: Janet Mills for Maine
About the Governor | Office of Governor Janet T. Mills - Maine.gov

Economy.stance: Mills emphasizes a pro-middle-class economic agenda centered on jobs, housing, childcare, broadband, and workforce development. Her campaign and governor biography describe her as focused on good-paying jobs and affordability.

sources.title: Janet Mills for Maine
About the Governor | Office of Governor Janet T. Mills - Maine.gov

Climate/Energy.stance: Mills supports Maine's climate and clean-energy agenda and has backed state efforts to expand renewable electricity and electrification. Her administration has framed climate policy as part of broader economic and energy planning.

sources.title: Energy, Environment, and Climate Change - Maine.gov
Mills announces new Maine climate action plan

Reproductive Rights.stance: Mills supports abortion access and has signed legislation protecting reproductive health care in Maine. Her public record places her on the abortion-rights side of the issue.

sources.title: Gov. Mills Signs Bill to Expand and Protect Abortion Access
Janet Mills for Maine

Immigration.stance: Mills has defended immigrants and refugees in Maine and has opposed broad local cooperation with federal immigration enforcement. She has said Maine will not be intimidated by federal immigration operations.

sources.title: Governor Mills Statement on Federal Law Enforcement Operations in Maine
Janet Mills: Maine 'will not be intimidated' amid ongoing immigration enforcement

Guns & Safety.stance: Mills has called for common-sense gun-safety measures after the Lewiston mass shooting and has said Maine needs a serious conversation about preventing gun violence. Her public posture supports stronger gun-safety laws and related reforms.

sources.title: Practical, Common-Sense Measures to Keep Maine People Safe
Gov. Janet Mills ready to hear proposals to further gun safety

Foreign Policy.stance: Mills has criticized unilateral military action and said Congress should authorize decisions to go to war. She has also condemned recent strikes on Iran as unconstitutional and illegal.

sources.title: Janet Mills says Iran strikes unconstitutional, illegal

Social Justice.stance: Mills has emphasized reproductive rights and LGBTQ equality in her public record. She has opposed efforts she says would restrict transgender rights and has framed her agenda around rights and opportunity for all Mainers.

sources.title: Gov. Mills Signs Bill to Expand and Protect Abortion Access
Maine Gov. Mills Opposes Referendum That Would Ban Transgender Girls From High School Sports

Education.stance: Mills supports fully funding K-12 education, raising teacher pay, and keeping community college tuition-free for recent high school graduates. She also backs early childhood education, child care, school construction, and career and technical education.

sources.title: Education | Office of Governor Janet T. Mills
In final budget, Gov. Mills proposes tapping more than $300 million from rainy day fund

Tech & AI.stance: Mills has taken a governance-focused approach to artificial intelligence, creating a state AI task force and backing recommendations on AI literacy, privacy, transparency, and responsible deployment. Her administration presents AI policy as a mix of innovation and safeguards.

sources.title: Governor Mills Signs Executive Order Establishing Task Force on Artificial Intelligence
AI Task Force | Office of Policy Innovation & Future - Maine.gov

Election Reform.stance: Mills opposes voter-ID-style election restrictions and has defended Maine's absentee-friendly election system. After a 2025 ballot measure failed, she said Mainers rejected an attempt to restrict voting rights.

sources.title: Governor Mills Statement on Rejection of Question 1
Gov. Mills speaks out against Republican-backed SAVE Act

Local Issues.stance: Mills' local agenda centers on affordability, including housing expansion, relief for households, lower utility and education costs, and continued access to free community college. She has also proposed using state reserves to finance new housing and provide direct relief.

sources.title: Affordability Agenda – Governor Mills (PDF)
In Final State of the State, Governor Mills Unveils Affordability Agenda

candidates.donor_summary: Janet T. Mills’ public finance footprint is primarily from state-level disclosures and gubernatorial fundraising, not a long federal donor record. OpenSecrets and FollowTheMoney show her state-officeholder profile, while campaign reporting indicates her Senate campaign raised about $2.7M in its first quarter and later about $2.7M in a subsequent quarter; the best full donor data source is FollowTheMoney or her campaign/FEC filings. Sources: https://www.followthemoney.org/entity-details?eid=9941379 ; https://www.opensecrets.org/officeholders/janet-t-mills/summary?cycle=2021&id=9941379 ; https://janetmills.com/governor-janet-mills-campaign-for-u-s-senate-raises-2-7-million-in-first-fundraising-quarter-of-candidacy/ ; https://www.mainepublic.org/politics/2025-07-15/gubernatorial-and-congressional-campaigns-rake-in-cash-as-2026-elections-ramp-up

Office of Governor Janet T. Mills - Maine.gov

candidates.voting_summary: Janet T. Mills has not held federal legislative office, but she has extensive executive and state-legislative experience: governor of Maine, former attorney general, and former Maine House member. Her comparable governing record includes signing state legislation such as medical-debt protections and other policy actions as governor; Vote Smart also tracks her issue positions and state-level record. Sources: http://www.maine.gov/governor/mills/ ; http://www.maine.gov/governor/mills/about ; https://justfacts.votesmart.org/candidate/key-votes/36856/janet-mills ; https://justfacts.votesmart.org/candidate/biography/36856/janet-mills

candidates.name: Graham Platner
candidates.summary: Graham Platner is a Democratic candidate for U.S. Senate in Maine, where he is running in the 2026 primary. He is an oyster farmer and military veteran, and his campaign has emphasized economic populism, health care, and opposition to corporate influence in politics.

summary_sources.title: Platform — Graham Platner | Democrat for U.S. Senate
Who is Graham Platner and why is he everywhere right now?

career_history.title: Oyster farmer
career_history.description: Works as an oyster farmer in Maine.

career_history.title: Harbormaster
career_history.description: Has been described as a harbormaster in reporting and biographical coverage.

career_history.title: Defense contractor
career_history.description: Worked as a defense contractor after military service.

career_history.title: Bartender
career_history.description: Worked as a bartender after leaving active duty.

career_history.title: U.S. Marine Corps / Army National Guard
career_history.description: Served three tours in Iraq as a Marine and later another tour in Afghanistan with the Army National Guard.

source.title: REPORT OF RECEIPTS AND DISBURSEMENTS - FEC
REPORT OF RECEIPTS AND DISBURSEMENTS - FEC
REPORT OF RECEIPTS AND DISBURSEMENTS - FEC

Healthcare.stance: Platner advocates universal health coverage by implementing Medicare for All and moving away from the for-profit health-insurance model; he also emphasizes expanding access to mental health care and protecting rural hospitals. He supports government negotiation of prescription drug prices and breaking up health-care monopolies to reduce costs for working Mainers.

sources.title: U.S. Senate candidate Graham Platner lays out health care reforms (NewsCenterMaine)
Senate candidate Graham Platner pitches fully funded care to Maine voters (WGME)
Platner raised money with health care lobbyists before fundraising pledge (Bangor Daily News)

Economy.stance: Platner says Maine is being squeezed by a billionaire economy and argues for an economy that works for workers and small businesses rather than wealthy elites. His platform calls for raising the federal minimum wage, supporting unions, protecting working waterfront jobs, breaking up monopolies, and taxing billionaires more heavily.

sources.title: Platform — Graham Platner | Democrat for U.S. Senate

Climate/Energy.stance: Platner says he will fight for urgent action on climate change, strengthen environmental protections, and restore a stronger EPA. He also says Maine’s fisheries, lobstermen, and farmers are already affected by climate change and that he wants solutions that protect those industries without unnecessary burdens.

sources.title: Platform — Graham Platner | Democrat for U.S. Senate

Reproductive Rights.stance: Platner supports a strong federal guarantee of the right to choose and says government should stay out of private reproductive decisions. His platform explicitly backs abortion rights rather than restrictions.

sources.title: Platform — Graham Platner | Democrat for U.S. Senate

Immigration.stance: Platner has taken a strongly anti-ICE position, saying ICE is not welcome in communities and criticizing federal immigration enforcement as authoritarian overreach. His public comments indicate support for protecting immigrants and resisting aggressive deportation operations.

sources.title: Platner: 'ICE is not welcome in our communities' - The Hill
We have all heard the disturbing news: ICE is very likely coming for Maine...

Foreign Policy.stance: Platner takes a sharply anti-interventionist stance and has criticized U.S. support for Israel, including calling the war in Gaza a genocide and urging the U.S. to use its leverage to end it. He also opposed U.S.-Israeli military action against Iran and framed it as dangerous escalation.

sources.title: Platform — Graham Platner | Democrat for U.S. Senate
Senate candidate Graham Platner joins Portland anti-war rally
Graham Platner, others protest Iran military action outside Sen. Susan Collins' Portland office

Social Justice.stance: Graham Platner's platform explicitly supports federal LGBTQ anti-discrimination legislation to protect equal rights and dignity for LGBTQ Americans; he frames equality for all as a core principle and commits to advancing civil rights protections at the federal level.

sources.title: Platform - Graham Platner

Education.stance: Platner says the federal government should ensure every school district has adequate resources to provide a quality public education, and he opposes federal support for private school vouchers.

sources.title: Platform — Graham Platner | Democrat for U.S. Senate

Tech & AI.stance: Platner says corporations should be restricted from using AI to surveil, track, and subject Americans to predatory pricing practices. His broader platform emphasizes consumer protection and limiting corporate abuse.

sources.title: Platform — Graham Platner | Democrat for U.S. Senate

Election Reform.stance: Platner supports a constitutional amendment to ban billionaires from buying elections and says Citizens United has enabled wealthy donors to dominate politics. His platform frames campaign-finance reform as central to restoring democracy.

sources.title: Platform — Graham Platner | Democrat for U.S. Senate

Local Issues.stance: Platner prioritizes Maine’s working waterfront, fisheries, and maritime workforce, including expanded cold storage, grant programs, invasive species controls, and support for the Working Waterfronts Act. He also says he will fight to fund rural hospitals and protect access to care in Maine communities.

sources.title: Platform — Graham Platner | Democrat for U.S. Senate

candidates.donor_summary: Graham Platner’s fundraising has been driven by small-dollar grassroots giving, with campaign and news reports showing roughly $1M raised shortly after launch and later about $4.6M to $4.7M in a quarter. Available reporting suggests the donor base is dominated by individual contributions rather than corporate PAC money, with the campaign emphasizing anti-corporate and anti-lobbyist fundraising; the best full donor data source is his FEC page. Sources: https://www.fec.gov/data/candidate/S6ME00373/ ; https://www.mainepublic.org/politics/2026-02-02/graham-platner-posts-4-6-million-in-fourth-quarter-fundraising-gov-janet-mills-2-7-million ; https://www.axios.com/2026/01/06/graham-platner-maine-senate-fundraising ; https://www.mainemorningstar.com/2025/08/28/the-oysterman-trying-to-oust-susan-collins-raised-1-million-in-nine-days/ ; https://www.bangordailynews.com/2026/03/30/politics/elections/graham-platner-raised-money-lobbyists-before-fundraising-pledge-joam40zk0w/

links.title: Graham Platner | Democrat for U.S. Senate
Platform — Graham Platner | Democrat for U.S. Senate
Who is Graham Platner and why is he everywhere right now?
Graham Platner posts $4.6 million in fourth quarter fundraising, Gov. Janet Mills $2.7 million
Exclusive: Platner raises $5 million despite scandals - Axios

candidates.voting_summary: Graham Platner has not held legislative office, so there is no legislative voting record to report. Comparable evidence of his governing approach is limited to campaign policy statements and public pledges, including his platform and issue positions on his campaign site and a reported pledge on health-care lobbying. Sources: https://www.grahamforsenate.com/ ; https://www.grahamforsenate.com/platform ; https://www.bangordailynews.com/2026/03/30/politics/elections/graham-platner-raised-money-lobbyists-before-fundraising-pledge-joam40zk0w/

candidates.name: Jordan Wood
candidates.summary: Jordan Wood is a Maine Democrat from Lewiston who is running for Congress after previously campaigning for the U.S. Senate. His campaign says he has worked on democracy reform, consumer protection, and anti-corruption efforts, and he now seeks to represent Maine's 2nd Congressional District.

summary_sources.title: Maine Democrat Jordan Wood ends Senate campaign, launches House run

career_history.title: Candidate for U.S. House, Maine District 2
career_history.description: Running for the Democratic nomination for Maine's 2nd Congressional District after ending his Senate bid.

career_history.title: Candidate for U.S. House, Maine District 2
career_history.description: Running for the Democratic nomination for Maine's 2nd Congressional District after ending his Senate bid.

Healthcare.stance: Supports a Medicare for All system, including a single-payer national health insurance model, while also defending the Affordable Care Act and expanding access to care in rural communities. He says the plan would eliminate deductibles, co-pays, and premiums for covered care and preserve access to private insurance for those who want it.

sources.title: Pass a Medicare for All System To Guarantee Coverage for All
About | Jordan Wood for Maine

Economy.stance: Supports lowering taxes for working families, raising taxes on ultra-millionaires, strengthening small businesses and unions, and using anti-corruption reforms to make the economy work for regular Mainers rather than corporations and lobbyists.

sources.title: Drastically Cut Taxes for Working Families, Lower Taxes to 1% for First $50,000 Earned
Issues | Jordan Wood for Maine

Climate/Energy.stance: Supports fighting climate change by strengthening environmental protections, opposing corporate polluters, and making renewable energy affordable. His campaign frames climate action as important for protecting Maine's land, water, forests, and coastal industries.

sources.title: Protect our Environment and Tackle the Climate Crisis | Jordan Wood for Maine

Reproductive Rights.stance: Supports restoring abortion rights nationwide, codifying Roe v. Wade into federal law, and increasing funding for Planned Parenthood and reproductive health care access.

sources.title: Restore Abortion Rights Nationwide | Jordan Wood for Maine

Immigration.stance: Supports fixing the immigration system by ending unlawful arrests without due process or warrants and requiring proper identification for officers involved in immigration enforcement. His campaign presents immigration as a due-process and civil-liberties issue.

sources.title: Fix Our Broken Immigration System | Jordan Wood for Maine

Guns & Safety.stance: Supports universal background checks, red flag laws, a ban on assault weapons, and increased mental health and school safety funding to reduce gun violence.

sources.title: Keep Our Communities Safe from Gun Violence | Jordan Wood for Maine

Foreign Policy.stance: Supports promoting democracy and human rights and pursuing peace through diplomacy. His campaign does not appear to offer a detailed country-by-country foreign policy agenda in the materials reviewed.

sources.title: Issues | Jordan Wood for Maine
Platform | Jordan Wood for Maine

Social Justice.stance: Supports LGBTQ+ equality, the Equality Act, tribal sovereignty, and abortion rights as part of a broader civil-rights agenda. His campaign says it would protect people from discrimination based on sexual orientation and gender identity.

sources.title: Champion LGBTQ+ Equality | Jordan Wood for Maine
Platform | Jordan Wood for Maine

Education.stance: Supports making need-based federal student loans interest-free, enacting free community college, expanding universal pre-K, and broadening apprenticeships and trade schools.

sources.title: Enact Free Community College for All | Jordan Wood for Maine
Issues | Jordan Wood for Maine

Tech & AI.stance: Supports regulating AI and algorithms when they are used for surveillance, tracking, predatory pricing, or rent fixing. His platform emphasizes consumer protection and anti-abuse rules rather than a pro-innovation technology agenda.

sources.title: Platform | Jordan Wood for Maine

Election Reform.stance: Supports overturning Citizens United, banning corporate PAC and lobbyist donations, ending dark money and partisan gerrymandering, protecting voting rights, and using public financing for elections.

sources.title: Platform | Jordan Wood for Maine

Local Issues.stance: Supports making housing more affordable by limiting corporate landlord purchases, curbing rent-price algorithms, accelerating new housing construction, and investing in rural broadband and infrastructure.

sources.title: Make Housing Affordable for Buyers and Renters
Platform | Jordan Wood for Maine

candidates.donor_summary: Jordan Wood’s fundraising appears heavily grassroots and anti-corporate, with his campaign publicly saying it raised about $810K in the first quarter and later announcing $1.2M raised. The campaign also says it took no corporate PAC or lobbyist money and reported an average donation of about $19, indicating a small-dollar donor base rather than industry-dominated support. Sources: https://electjordan.com/jordan-wood-announces-1-2-million-raised/ ; https://www.facebook.com/jordanwoodmaine/posts/810000-raised-average-donation-19-not-a-single-dollar-from-a-corporate-pac-or-lo/122166060944853503/ ; https://www.fec.gov/data/candidate/S6ME00332/ ; https://www.opensecrets.org/campaign-expenditures/vendor?year=2024&vendor=Wood%2C%20Jordan

links.title: Jordan Wood (@jordanwoodmaine) • Instagram photos and videos
Maine Democrat Jordan Wood ends Senate campaign, launches House run

candidates.voting_summary: Jordan Wood has not held legislative office, so there is no legislative voting record to report. Comparable evidence of his governing approach is his campaign platform and prior political work, which emphasize anti-corruption reform and opposition to corporate PAC influence rather than a record of roll-call votes. Sources: https://electjordan.com/ ; https://electjordan.com/platform/ ; https://www.opensecrets.org/revolving-door/wood-jordan/summary?id=82324

flags.concern: The University of New Hampshire poll dated 2026-02-16 links to Ballotpedia as secondary source aggregation rather than the direct UNH Survey Center release. While Ballotpedia is credible, direct polling releases are preferable for verification.
flags.suggestion: If available, replace with direct UNH Survey Center URL (e.g., scholars.unh.edu) for the specific 2026-02-16 poll.

flags.concern: The Insurance Watchdog Coalition / OnMessage poll also links to Ballotpedia rather than a direct source. No original URL is provided for this commissioned poll.
flags.suggestion: Verify whether an original press release or direct poll release exists from OnMessage or the commissioning organization, or note that the poll is accessed via secondary aggregation.

flags.concern: Janet Mills' top donor entry lists 'N A' with $1,889,925 and no organization field. The second entry is 'Self Employed' with $473,275 and no org. These appear to be aggregated categories from state-level disclosure data rather than discrete donors, which may obscure actual funding sources.
flags.suggestion: Clarify in the donor_summary that these are aggregated self-identified categories from Maine state filings, not named entities. Include a note that OpenSecrets state-officeholder data has different structures than federal candidate FEC filings.

flags.concern: Graham Platner's top-donor list shows three entries with minimal amounts ($500, $5,000, $200). The sample size is very small relative to reported total fundraising ($4.6M in Q4), suggesting the FEC reports cited may not capture the full grassroots donor base. This is not inaccurate, but the imbalance may be misleading.
flags.suggestion: Add a brief note to the donor_summary explaining that Platner's grassroots fundraising (many small donations) is under-represented in the 'top_donors' list, which captures only the highest individual/entity amounts. Link directly to the FEC page or note the data source limitation.

flags.concern: Jordan Wood's career history lists 'Candidate for U.S. House, Maine District 2' twice, identically (2025–present). This appears to be a duplicate entry or data-import error.
flags.suggestion: Remove the duplicate entry or clarify if there were two distinct candidacy announcements with different timelines.

flags.concern: The voting record entry for 'Confirmation of Markwayne Mullin as Secretary of Homeland Security' has a date listed as '2025-01-XX' (placeholder month/day). This appears to be an incomplete date field.
flags.suggestion: Fill in the correct date (2025-01-13 based on typical DHS confirmation timelines) or clarify why the exact date is unavailable.

flags.concern: Janet Mills' career history shows she served as Attorney General 2009–2011 and then 'later returned to the office from 2013 to 2019.' The profile does not specify what she did between 2011–2013, creating a minor gap in the career timeline.
flags.suggestion: Clarify whether she held other office or private practice between 2011–2013, or cite a source that explains the gap.

reviews.summary: This is a well-researched and comprehensively sourced profile of Maine's 2026 Senate race. Strengths include: (1) Strong sourcing across all four candidates with official campaign sites, news outlets, and government records cited; (2) Detailed issue positions for each candidate with high-confidence ratings and credible sources—particularly robust on Susan Collins and Janet Mills, with substantial cited official pages; (3) Career histories and education sections are internally consistent with cited sources; (4) Polling data clearly attributed with dates and sample sizes; (5) Neutral, nonpartisan language throughout, with candidate positions presented factually without editorial framing; (6) Appropriate handling of gaps—e.g., Graham Platner's 'Guns & Safety' marked 'low confidence' with no public position found, and Jordan Wood's 'Foreign Policy' marked 'medium confidence' with transparent sourcing limitations. Minor areas for strengthening: some polling URLs (Ballotpedia aggregations) are secondary sources rather than direct pollster releases, and a few top-donor entries have null amounts or vague categorizations (e.g., 'N A' for Janet Mills). These do not undermine the overall quality of the research.

flags.concern: The 'Emerson College Polling' entry dated 2026-03-26 is duplicated in the polling array with identical sample sizes and matchups.
flags.suggestion: Remove the duplicate entry to ensure the polling aggregate is not double-counted.

flags.concern: Jordan Wood's career history contains two identical entries for 'Candidate for U.S. House, Maine District 2'.
flags.suggestion: Consolidate the duplicate career history entries.

flags.concern: The profile references news articles and events dated in 2026 (e.g., Collins' reelection announcement in February 2026). My training data cannot independently verify events occurring in the future — these source URLs should be confirmed by a human reviewer to ensure they are live and accurate citations.

The UNH Survey Center poll (Article 1929) cited for 2026 matchups historically refers to 2020 election data in other contexts. Please verify the source content directly to ensure it supports the 2026 matchups described.
flags.suggestion: Confirm that the document at the URL contains the specific 2026 primary and general election projections mentioned.

reviews.summary: This research profile is exceptionally thorough and demonstrates high internal consistency. It provides deep coverage of 12 distinct policy issues for all major candidates, including specific stances and confidence ratings. Career histories are detailed and align well with the provided source documentation. The inclusion of legislative voting records for incumbents and candidates with state-level experience (Collins and Mills) is a significant strength, as is the detailed fundraising data for all candidates, including primary challengers.

flags.concern: There is a duplicate entry for 'Candidate for U.S. House, Maine District 2', which may indicate a formatting or data entry oversight in the profile.
flags.suggestion: Remove the duplicate entry to improve clarity and avoid redundancy.

reviews.summary: This profile is a well-constructed and thoroughly sourced overview of the 2026 Maine Senate race, with strong internal consistency across candidate summaries, issues, and polling data, drawing from credible sources like official government sites, campaign pages, and reputable news outlets. It effectively balances detailed career histories and issue stances with appropriate notes on gaps, such as 'no public position found' for certain topics, demonstrating a fair and comprehensive effort.

polling_note: Recent polling has focused on the Democratic primary and early general-election matchups. The most recent publicly reported survey located in this update was the University of New Hampshire Survey Center poll released after 2026-04-06, which showed Graham Platner leading Janet Mills in the Democratic primary and narrow general-election margins against Susan Collins.

validation_grade.summary: Validated by 3/3 reviewers with an average score of 91/100.
